intent: For each AI research lab profiled in the SearchOS demo corpus — Nimbus Labs, Orchard AI, Basilisk Research, and Tidewater Institute — find its founding year, headquarters city, and flagship open model. Present the results as a table with a citation for every cell.

questions.task_prompt: Survey the SearchOS demo corpus to identify the correct scope of 'AI research labs' as listed: Nimbus Labs, Orchard AI, Basilisk Research, and Tidewater Institute. For each, clarify entity name variants if any, confirm that all four are in scope, and flag any that are not profiled in the demo corpus. For each valid entity, determine the required attributes: founding year (format: YYYY), headquarters city (city only, not country), and flagship open model (official designation or codename, most representative open AI model released by the lab). Flag any attribute that is ambiguous or missing in the corpus. Suggest a table structure with the minimal primary key and the precise column types/formats as required.

questions.question: Extract the founding year (YYYY), headquarters city (city only), and flagship open model (official codename/designation) for Nimbus Labs from its dedicated profile and the canonical directory in the S
questions.task_prompt: Extract the founding year (YYYY), headquarters city (city only), and flagship open model (official codename/designation) for Nimbus Labs from its dedicated profile and the canonical directory in the SearchOS demo corpus. Provide a citation URL for EACH value (preferably the directory or https://nimbus-labs.example/about). If the flagship model is not listed on the profile, check the lab's flagship model profile page. questions.resolution:
- Founding year: 2019 (from https://nimbus-labs.example/about)
- Headquarters city: Reykjavik (from https://nimbus-labs.example/contact)
- Flagship open model: StormCast-3 (from https://nimbus-labs.example/models)

Trail: Searched for the Nimbus Labs profile, directory, and flagship model; read the 'about', 'contact', and 'models' official pages. Each value is taken directly from the dedicated citation as requested.

Gaps: No gaps; all requested values were found on Nimbus Labs’ official site and cited per cell.

questions.resolution:
- Orchard AI's founding year is 2021, and its headquarters city is Lisbon, according to its official profile at https://orchard-ai.example/about.
- The flagship open model for Orchard AI is "Pippin-1B" per the dedicated flagship model page: https://orchard-ai.example/flagship.

Sources:
- Founding year: 2021 — https://orchard-ai.example/about
- Headquarters city: Lisbon — https://orchard-ai.example/about
- Flagship model: Pippin-1B — https://orchard-ai.example/flagship

Trail: Searched and directly read the dedicated "about" page, the canonical labs directory index, and the flagship model's official page for confirmation.
Gaps: None; all target facts were present and clearly attributed to authoritative lab-owned sources.

questions.resolution:
- Tidewater Institute founding year: 2016 (per both the official profile at https://tidewater-institute.example/profile and the canonical directory listing at https://ai-labs-directory.example/labs-index).
- Headquarters city: Hobart (city), Australia (per both sources: https://tidewater-institute.example/profile and https://ai-labs-directory.example/labs-index).
- Flagship open model: Ebb-Sim (official designation, confirmed as the headline open-source release on the Tidewater Institute profile at https://tidewater-institute.example/profile; also referenced in the search results as the flagship model).

Trail: Searched for and checked the Tidewater Institute official profile and the canonical directory. Also reviewed the dedicated flagship model result to confirm the model name and status.

All values are directly cited to the requested profile page (https://tidewater-institute.example/profile), which gives founding year, headquarters, and flagship model. The canonical directory (https://ai-labs-directory.example/labs-index) supports the founding year and headquarters city. No conflicts found.

pending_agent_summaries.content:
## 1. Query type & interpreted intent

**Type:** Enumeration/verification with attribute extraction (multi-entity profile setup).

**Intent:** 
- Survey the SearchOS demo corpus to confirm the precise in-scope set of 'AI research labs' from a user-supplied list: Nimbus Labs, Orchard AI, Basilisk Research, and Tidewater Institute.
- For each lab:
  - Clarify name variants (as used in the corpus).
  - Verify full inclusion/profiling in the corpus. If any are not present, flag them.
  - For included labs, determine these attributes (and flag if ambiguous/missing):
    - **Founding year** (YYYY)
    - **Headquarters city** (city name only)
    - **Flagship open model** (official designation or codename, representing the main openly released AI model per lab)
- Suggest a minimal, typed table schema to capture this.

**Eligibility checklist for a valid entity:**
- Entity must be profiled in the demo corpus as an AI research lab (not a generic mention, not outside the context of lab profile pages).
- Must have a discrete profile or feature page with at least partial organization info.
- Must have at least one open model attributed to the lab.

## 2. Information landscape

**Sources and data format:**
- The canonical, comprehensive source is the "Open-Source AI Research Labs — Directory" ([directory link](https://ai-labs-directory.example/labs-index)), which lists the four entities directly, each with linked or embedded profile info. This source explicitly includes all four user-supplied names.
- Each lab also has a dedicated profile/overview or flagship model page (running text), providing standardized details: founding year, headquarters city, and the flagship open model.
- Attribute data is present in structured sentences; flagships are given as model names/codenames.
- Entity names and key attributes are always presented in running text, with no evidence of alternate name variants in the corpus.
- Coverage appears complete—no evidence of missing or unprofiled entities among the four.
- The corpus is self-contained and topical, with labs, attribute names, and models clearly delineated and findable.

**Data availability assessment:**
- Data is readily available for all required attributes except that model flags may sometimes require a supplemental specific model page.
- No attributes are found missing so far; model flagship names can be mapped (see below).
- No name ambiguity or alternate variants were found.
- All four requested labs are profiled (source: directory and lab-specific pages).

**Coverage ledger:**
- Waves completed: 2
  - Wave 1: Searched for the corpus-wide lab list, all four labs by name, attribute list queries, presence in summaries, and entity variant checks.
  - Wave 2: Targeted official flagship model names and explicit entity name variants confirmation per lab.
- Source classes checked: Canonical directory, individual lab profiles/model pages.
- Entities added: All four requested labs, no extras, no exclusions.
- All dimensions saturated; no measurable disagreement, no evidence of missing attributes.
- **Saturation test passed.**

**As-of temporal status:**  
- All corpus data is static (reflects organizations as profiled in SearchOS demo corpus; no time series or "as-of" date issues).  
- Founding years and flagship models are presented as point facts in current organizational profiles.

## 3. Recommended search strategy

- **Search queries to use for attribute extraction:**
  - "[Lab name] founding year"
  - "[Lab name] headquarters city"
  - "[Lab name] flagship model" (or "[Lab name] open model")
- Prioritize the canonical directory and direct lab profiles on their official pages ('.example' domains); these repeat and cross-validate each other.
- For possible edge cases (if user requests further validation): cross-check flagship model names with their dedicated model profile pages (e.g., "Nimbus Labs StormCast-3", "Orchard AI Pippin-1B", etc).
- No evidence of data scattered or paywalled; avoid general blogs/roundups—they do not provide definitive org attributes.

**How to partition the work:**  
- Work per entity/lab; for each lab, extract founding year, headquarters city, and flagship open model from its profile.
- Each attribute should be filled from the corpus primary source (directory or direct org/model profile).
- If gaps appear in primary sources, attempt to resolve them with targeted flaghsip model or overview/profile queries.

## 4. Candidate entities

**All entities both in-scope and fully profiled according to the corpus:**
- Nimbus Labs (from-page: https://ai-labs-directory.example/labs-index, https://nimbus-labs.example/about)
- Orchard AI (from-page: https://ai-labs-directory.example/labs-index, https://orchard-ai.example/about)
- Basilisk Research (from-page: https://ai-labs-directory.example/labs-index, https://basilisk-research.example/home)
- Tidewater Institute (from-page: https://ai-labs-directory.example/labs-index, https://tidewater-institute.example/profile)
No evidence of alternate variants or substitute names in the corpus.

## 5. Table structure suggestion

**Recommended table schema (minimal required):**

| Column Name            | Data Type    | Constraints                       | Description                                  |
|------------------------|-------------|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| lab_name               | VARCHAR     | PRIMARY KEY (canonical, case-insensitive) | Official organization name (canonical, corpus-used form)    |
| founding_year          | CHAR(4)     | YYYY, NOT NULL                    | Founding year, 4-digit ISO format            |
| headquarters_city      | VARCHAR     | NOT NULL                          | City (canonical, per corpus)                 |
| flagship_model         | VARCHAR     | NOT NULL                          | Official model codename/designation per corpus; no units |
  
- **Primary Key:** lab_name (no evidence of ambiguity or duplicates)
- All fields required per eligibility, but if an attribute is missing in the corpus, flag as NULL with a note in the output.
- No secondary or foreign keys needed (flat profile).

## 6. Ambiguity resolution

- **Name variants:** No corpus evidence found for alternate names or acronyms for any of the four labs; use corpus canonical names.
- **Attribute ambiguity:** None detected at this stage; all attributes available for all entities per corpus.
- **Scope exclusion:** All four supplied labs are in-scope and profiled; zero labs excluded for being out of scope.

---

**Summary:**  
- All four labs (Nimbus Labs, Orchard AI, Basilisk Research, Tidewater Institute) are in corpus scope as profiled AI research labs under their official names.
- No missing, ambiguous, or unprofiled entities among the list.
- Corpus lists: founding year (YYYY), headquarters city (string), flagship open model (string/codename) for each lab.
- Strongly recommend entity-oriented, single-table structure keyed on lab name, with minimal, required, typed columns as above.
